---
title: "nitrix.metrics.overlap"
generated: true
signature-hash: e2b8d4fcf5288519
description: "Region-overlap segmentation metrics."
listing:
  id: nitrix-metrics-overlap-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-metrics-overlap/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.metrics.overlap`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Region-overlap segmentation metrics.

- [`dice`](nitrix-metrics-overlap/dice.qmd) -- the soft Sørensen--Dice overlap coefficient: twice the
  intersection over the sum of magnitudes,
  $2 |A \cap B| / (|A| + |B|)$.
- [`jaccard`](nitrix-metrics-overlap/jaccard.qmd) -- the soft Jaccard index / intersection-over-union
  (IoU): the intersection over the union,
  $|A \cap B| / |A \cup B|$.

Both are evaluated on soft (probabilistic) masks rather than hard
labels, so they are differentiable and double as losses
($1 - \mathrm{metric}$).  They are monotonically related --
$\mathrm{jaccard} = \mathrm{dice} / (2 - \mathrm{dice})$ -- so
they rank a set of predictions identically, but report different
operating points (Jaccard is the stricter of the two for any imperfect
overlap); pipelines commonly track both.

Convention (shared with the intensity and information metrics): the
function returns the *similarity* (the coefficient, in $[0, 1]$);
form the cost as $1 - \mathrm{metric}$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-metrics-overlap-symbols}
:::
